Implement long press interactions and move ComponentIds to core (#517)

* Implement long press interactions and move ComponentIds to core

Add ars-interactions::long_press with adapter-facing state transitions, announcements, shared press cancellation, and expanded regression coverage. Rework press handling to support simultaneous modalities, restore spec-defined state transitions and callbacks, and tighten the remaining edge-case behavior identified during the spec review passes.

Move ComponentIds into ars-core, remove the unnecessary ars-a11y dependency from ars-forms, and sync the architecture, accessibility, interactions, forms, and testing specs to match the delivered APIs and contracts. Verified with cargo xci.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Consume long-press cancel state on every press release

Clear the shared long-press suppression flag even when the releasing modality is outside the element so the state cannot leak into a later concurrent release. Add a regression test covering the outside-release then keyboard-activation sequence reported in PR review.

Verified with cargo xci.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Scope long-press suppression to the firing modality

Replace the shared long-press boolean with shared modality state so Press only consumes suppression for the releasing modality that actually fired LongPress. Update the interaction spec examples and composition contract to match, and add regression coverage for cross-modality release ordering.

Verified with cargo xci.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Clear long-press suppression on cancel

Clear the modality-scoped long-press suppression state when cancel_press removes the matching modality so stale suppression cannot leak into a later fresh press. Add regression coverage for the cancelled-long-press then fresh-press sequence.

Verified with cargo xci.

[NSM] Phase 1 Step 4: fork llama.rs onto StateRegistry trait (#4)

* [NSM] Phase 1 Step 4 / Phase 0: golden-token gate for SmolLM2-135M

Captures the first 20 argmax tokens from stock candle Llama::forward for a
fixed prompt ("The capital of France is") and asserts regeneration matches
byte-for-byte. This is the Step 5 equivalence oracle pulled forward — once
Step 4 swaps in the StateRegistry-backed fork, this test is the gate that
catches any divergence in the forward pass.

- crates/nsm-core/tests/golden_smollm2.rs — integration test, #[ignore]
  because it needs network + ~270 MB of weights (cached after first pull)
- crates/nsm-core/tests/fixtures/smollm2_golden.json — committed fixture
  (20 tokens, F32, CPU)
- hf-hub + tokenizers added as dev-dependencies of nsm-core

Run: cargo test -p nsm-core --test golden_smollm2 -- --ignored
Regen: NSM_REGEN_GOLDEN=1 <same command> (only on intentional contract
change: model, prompt, dtype, or token count)

Default `cargo test -p nsm-core` still runs only the 5 unit tests (golden
test is correctly skipped as ignored). cargo clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [NSM] Phase 1 Step 4 / Phase A: STEP4_MAPPING.md design spec

Analysis-only deliverable. Specifies every Cache touchpoint in candle
0.9.2 llama.rs and how it maps into the fork's StateRegistry-backed
variant. Locks in design decisions BEFORE code changes so Phase B
(adversarial Codex review) and Phase D (surgery) have a precise spec.

Key decisions (all with rationale in the doc):

  - Cache struct keeps name + masks/cos/sin/device; kvs field and
    use_kv_cache bool are dropped. KV externalized to &mut S.

  - Generic <S: StateRegistry> attaches to forward METHODS only,
    not to Llama/Block/CausalSelfAttention structs. Minimal diff,
    no PhantomData, no lifetime params.

  - Mutability: &mut Cache (mask memoization) and &mut S (KV append)
    are threaded as separate parameters — two mutable borrows on
    distinct structs, no RefCell needed.

  - CacheKey { layer: block_idx, component: Key|Value } — two slots
    per layer instead of upstream's (Tensor, Tensor) tuple.

  - Access pattern: append(k) + append(v) + retrieve clones for local
    rebind. Trait's append encapsulates the Tensor::cat.

  - Upstream max_position_embeddings truncation block (lines 304-323)
    is DROPPED. Dead code in upstream (dims-index bug: dims()[1] is
    n_heads post-transpose, never > max_pos). Full rationale in §7.4.

  - Upstream use_kv_cache: false path is NOT preserved. Fork always
    uses registry; future NoOpStateRegistry impl can satisfy the
    trait if needed.

Prerequisite change identified for Phase D:
  - crates/nsm-core/src/state/stub.rs: add .contiguous()? after the
    Tensor::cat in StubStateRegistry::append, to match upstream's
    line 302-303 contiguity discipline. Existing unit tests cover.

Gemini flag cleared: candle 0.9.2 Cache struct has NOT drifted from
Phase 1 Step 0+3 assumptions (verified line-by-line).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [NSM] Phase 1 Step 4 / Phase B: apply Codex review findings to STEP4_MAPPING

Codex adversarial review (--effort high, read-only) returned 6 findings
against the Phase A spec. All accepted; all technically correct.

Severity breakdown:
  CRITICAL: none
  HIGH   : 1  (§7.4 "provably unreachable" language too absolute)
  MEDIUM : 2  (anyhow vs candle Result mismatch; Mlp<S> inconsistency)
  LOW    : 3  (contiguity test coverage, dyn ?Sized nit, NoOpRegistry claim)

Revisions applied:

  1. §5.1: removed Mlp from the list of methods gaining <S>. Mlp has no
     cache interaction; adding <S> would leave S uninferable at the
     Block::forward call site. §6's mapping table was already correct;
     §5.1 is now consistent with it.

  2. §5.2: clarified the dyn StateRegistry claim. Trait is object-safe,
     but <S: StateRegistry> carries an implicit Sized bound — switching
     to &mut dyn would need + ?Sized on every forward method. Noted
     purely for future reference.

  3. §5.5: rewrote the retrieve-error to use candle_core::Error::Msg
     instead of anyhow::anyhow!, and added an explicit "Error-type
     interop" subsection explaining why the trait's Result type must
     change to candle_core::Result before Phase D can compile.

  4. §6: corrected the use_kv_cache=false removal rationale. A NoOp
     registry does NOT cleanly satisfy the dropped path — §5.5's
     retrieve-after-append would hard-fail on None, whereas upstream's
     use_kv_cache=false branch skips write-back entirely. Restoring the
     mode is a forward-method change, not a trait swap. Out of Phase 1.

  5. §7.2: clarified that test_append_concatenates_seq_dim only asserts
     shape, not contiguity. Prerequisite edit now also adds an
     assert!(retrieved.is_contiguous()) so the change is
     regression-covered.

  6. §7.4: tightened the dead-code drop rationale. "Provably unreachable
     for any real model config" → "unreachable for every realistic
     decoder LLM (n_kv_heads in single digits to low tens;
     max_position_embeddings 2048+)". Acknowledged pathological configs
     where the branch fires, but noted upstream itself errors on them
     (narrow on head_dim=1). Step 5 is scoped to SmolLM2-135M.

Prerequisite change list (§11) expanded from 1 to 2 edits:
  - NEW: change StateRegistry::append return type to candle_core::Result
  - UPDATED: stub.rs now also needs error-type update + contiguity test

Both prerequisite edits land in the same Phase D PR as the llama.rs
fork — not a separate PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex (GPT-5.4 high-effort review) <codex@openai.com>

* [NSM] Phase 1 Step 4 / Phase C: verbatim fork of candle llama.rs

Copy candle-transformers 0.9.2 src/models/llama.rs into
crates/nsm-core/src/model/llama.rs with minimal import fixes only.
Establishes the clean diff baseline before Phase D surgery.

Changes vs upstream (diff verified minimal):
  - Added vendoring header (provenance + license + phase context).
  - `candle::{...}` → `candle_core::{...}` (candle-transformers
    aliases candle-core as `candle`; we use the crate directly).
  - `crate::utils::repeat_kv` → `super::utils::repeat_kv` (our utils
    sits at crate::model::utils, next to this file).

Workspace touches:
  - model/mod.rs registers `pub mod llama` with #[allow(dead_code)]
    (consumers wire up in Phase E).
  - nsm-core Cargo.toml declares `features.flash-attn = []` to silence
    `unexpected_cfgs` warnings from upstream's flash-attn gates. No
    candle-flash-attn dep; enabling the feature would require it.
  - THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md lists the new vendored file.

Verified:
  - cargo check -p nsm-core → clean
  - cargo clippy -p nsm-core → clean, no warnings
  - cargo test -p nsm-core → 5 unit tests pass, golden test ignored
    as expected (still pinned to stock candle until Phase E)
  - diff against upstream shows only the three intended changes

No behavior change: the module compiles but has no callers yet. The
StateRegistry-backed surgery (struct trimming, <S> threading, append-
then-retrieve pattern) lands in Phase D per docs/STEP4_MAPPING.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [NSM] Phase 1 Step 4 / Phase D: StateRegistry surgery on forked llama.rs

Externalize the KV cache from upstream candle's monolithic `Cache`
into the `StateRegistry` trait. Three forward methods gain a
`<S: StateRegistry>` generic + `registry: &mut S` parameter. Full
design spec and decisions in docs/STEP4_MAPPING.md.

Prerequisite edits (§11 of mapping doc):

  crates/nsm-core/src/state/registry.rs:
    - Trait `append` return type: anyhow::Result<()> → candle_core::Result<()>.
      Required so `?` composes naturally with candle's forward-pass APIs
      (see §5.5 of mapping doc).

  crates/nsm-core/src/state/stub.rs:
    - Switched error types from anyhow to candle_core::Error.
    - `.contiguous()?` added after `Tensor::cat` in
      StubStateRegistry::append, matching upstream candle's post-cat
      layout discipline (llama.rs 302-303 in candle-transformers 0.9.2).
    - `test_append_concatenates_seq_dim` now asserts is_contiguous() so
      the contiguity invariant is regression-covered.

Surgery on crates/nsm-core/src/model/llama.rs:

  Cache struct:
    - Dropped `pub use_kv_cache: bool` and
      `kvs: Vec<Option<(Tensor, Tensor)>>` fields.
    - Docstring explains the reduced scope (RoPE tables + mask memo
      + device; KV lives externally).
    - `Cache::new` signature no longer takes `use_kv_cache`.

  Generic threading (static dispatch only, structs stay non-generic):
    - CausalSelfAttention::forward<S>
    - Block::forward<S>
    - Llama::forward<S>
    - Llama::forward_input_embed<S>
    All take `registry: &mut S` alongside `&mut Cache`. Two mutable
    borrows are fine because Cache and S are distinct structs.

  KV read/write replacement (upstream lines 300-326):
    - `if cache.use_kv_cache { if let Some((cache_k, cache_v)) = ... }`
      block removed entirely.
    - Replaced with per-component append-then-retrieve pattern using
      CacheKey { layer: block_idx, component: Key|Value }. Trait's
      append() encapsulates the Tensor::cat; caller retrieves and
      clones to rebind local `k`/`v` to the full history.
    - Error on post-append retrieve miss uses candle_core::Error::Msg
      (never fires in practice; present because trait signature is
      Option<&Tensor>).

  Dead-code drop (upstream lines 304-323):
    - `max_position_embeddings` truncation branch removed with inline
      comment citing §7.4 of the mapping doc. Branch is unreachable
      for every realistic decoder-LLM config (n_kv_heads never exceeds
      max_position_embeddings), and upstream's `narrow(D::Minus1, ...)`
      trims head_dim rather than seq anyway.
    - `D` removed from imports (was the only user).

Verification:
  - cargo check -p nsm-core: clean
  - cargo clippy -p nsm-core --all-targets: clean, no warnings
  - cargo test -p nsm-core: 5 state tests pass (incl. new
    is_contiguous assert); golden_smollm2 still ignored (kernel
    hasn't been rewired yet — that's Phase E)
  - cargo check --workspace: clean (nsm-kernel still compiles
    against stock candle; Phase E switches it over)

Diff vs upstream is exactly the scope documented in the header comment
of llama.rs — no surprise changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [NSM] Phase 1 Step 4 / Phase E: address PR #4 review findings

Iteration 1 fixes from parallel review agents (code-reviewer,
silent-failure-hunter, pr-test-analyzer, type-design-analyzer):

Critical
- llama.rs Llama::load: replace .unwrap() in block load with
  Result<Vec<_>>?. Restores upstream's fallible-load behavior and
  gives callers an actionable candle error on missing/broken weights
  instead of a thread panic.
- stub.rs StubStateRegistry::append: add eager validate_for_append
  guard — rank-4 check, registry/tensor device match, and (when an
  existing slot exists) dtype + (batch,heads,head_dim) compatibility
  check. Turns silent KV-cache corruption (wrong-rank / cross-device
  / swapped-key writes) into localized errors at the boundary
  instead of unclear matmul failures layers later.

Important
- llama.rs: drop dead CausalSelfAttention.max_position_embeddings
  field (reader was the upstream truncation branch, already dropped
  in Phase D per STEP4_MAPPING §7.4).
- llama.rs: enrich cache-miss error with index_pos + seq_len plus
  a pointer to the two likely causes (append didn't persist / a
  clear() ran concurrently).
- golden_smollm2.rs: no longer silently forge the fixture on
  missing file — require explicit NSM_REGEN_GOLDEN=1. Also assert
  token_count vs GOLDEN_TOKEN_COUNT and tokens.len() bound.
- golden_smollm2.rs: ensure!() non-empty prompt tokens to avoid
  opaque underflow panic on an empty PROMPT.
- stub.rs: non-CPU device error now includes the rejected Device.

Tests added
- stub::test_append_preserves_dtype (BF16 round-trip)
- stub::test_key_and_value_are_distinct_slots (K/V aliasing guard)
- stub::test_append_rejects_rank_3_tensor (validation coverage)
- stub::test_append_rejects_incompatible_second_tensor (shape mismatch)
- golden::forked_llama_matches_golden (#[ignore]'d end-to-end parity
  test — runs nsm-core::model::llama::Llama<StubStateRegistry>
  through the same argmax loop and asserts byte-for-byte token
  equality with the committed fixture. Closes the test-coverage
  gap flagged by pr-test-analyzer: the fork was untested.)

cargo check / clippy --all-targets / test --lib all green. 9/9
stub unit tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(events): include user geo in formatted output (#894)

Include `user.geo` in formatted event output for both
`get_sentry_resource` event lookups and `search_issue_events` results.
This exposes the same location context already present in the upstream
event payload instead of forcing callers back to raw REST API responses.

The change also aligns our event schema with upstream Sentry
serialization by accepting nullable `user` and `user.geo` values. That
keeps parsing compatible with real events that do not have an attached
user while still surfacing geo data when it is present.

I considered handling this only in the formatter layer, but the event
parser also needed to understand the upstream payload shape or the fix
would remain unsafe for events with `user: null`.

Fixes #893

commit.message:
test: expand core coverage and harden helper behavior

Add direct unit coverage for the startup, backend, dispatcher, formatting, quoting, store, and command-bus paths that previously had little or no package-level protection. Tighten the weakest assertions in the generated command-help and tmux option catalog tests so they fail on real drift instead of only checking that data exists.

The new tests exposed two concrete issues in production code: table formatting assumed every row had the same number of columns and could panic on ragged input, and session-store cloning preserved shared backing slices for nested client lists. Fix those helpers so the test suite is protecting real behavior rather than just increasing coverage numbers.

Use narrow package-level seams in main and internal/app to exercise startup and resurrect-configuration branches without changing runtime behavior or turning the code into a DI-heavy design. Record the test-overhaul follow-up in context/done.md so the branch history reflects what changed and why.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(test): 让 IR 成为主测试契约并补齐递归嵌套快照 (#30)

## 为什么

- 现有大部分测试还在直接断言最终 ANSI/output，回归面过于贴近 terminal 编码，导致 runtime
复用、嵌套结构、block 几何和 render state 这些真正稳定的语义层很难直接验证。
- 目前的 `analysis -> visual -> render_ops -> terminal` 分层已经基本成形，但 nested
IR 还缺少递归子区域与子 runtime 身份，测试也还没有把 IR 作为主契约层用起来。

## 做了什么

- 给 analysis snapshot 补上 nested region 的 `resolved_document_kind` 和递归
`child_nested_regions`，让宿主感知注入、方言分发和 runtime 复用可以直接在 IR 上断言。
- 抽出 analysis / visual / render plan 级 helper，把 fixture/showcase 套件和一批嵌套
/ block 几何 / runtime 复用测试迁到 `analysis` / `render_ops` 断言，只保留最小必要的 ANSI /
terminal 边界回归。
- 同步更新 `README`、`docs/architecture.md`、`docs/test-assets.md` 和
`docs/roadmap.md`，把“IR 是主测试契约，ANSI 只守 terminal 边界”写成仓库约定。

## 验证

- `cargo test --lib`
- `cargo clippy --quiet --tests -- -D warnings`
- `KAT_AGENT_TEST_LOG_MODE=quiet just test`

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: 修复宽字符 HTML block tint 锯齿并收口显示几何 (#29)

## 原因
- `RectBlock` 一类块级 tint 之前混用了源码 UTF-8 偏移和终端显示列宽，遇到中文内容时会把统一右边界算错。
- 这种问题不是 Markdown HTML block 独有；所有依赖矩形 padding 的共享 block 路径理论上都有同类风险。

## 变更
- 引入共享 `display_geometry` 模块，统一处理 Unicode 显示宽度、tab stop 和相关显示列计算。
- 在显示几何层显式区分 `ByteOffset` 与 `DisplayColumn`，减少后续继续把裸 `usize` 混用成两种语义的空间。
- 把 `RectBlock` 的右边界归一化改成基于显示列宽计算，而不是按字节长度近似。
- 保留原有 Markdown HTML table 最小 fixture，并新增带 tab 的 HTML block fixture。
- 补充 GitHub Actions `run` 的宽字符回归，证明这次修复覆盖的是共享 block 路径，不是单点特判。

- 当前渲染链路把 analysis、visual、ANSI 编码和 terminal 能力探测混在一起，导致问题定位只能在
AST、最终终端效果和原始 ANSI 之间来回跳，缺少稳定的中间观察层。
- 仓库当前更需要可维护的分层边界、稳定的中间表示和可回归的 debug 出口，而不是继续在单体 renderer 上累积临时排查手段。

- 把渲染主链明确拆成 `analysis -> visual -> render_ops -> terminal` 四层，并保留
analysis 内部的 detect / highlight / semantic / injections 细分，而不是重新揉平成单层逻辑。
- 新增稳定 JSON debug
出口：`--debug-analysis`、`--debug-visual`、`--debug-render-ops`、`--debug-terminal`，同时把终端能力探测从
`theme` 中拆出，收口到独立 `terminal` 层。
- 让最终 ANSI 输出通过 `RenderPlan` 编码，补充新的 debug/CLI 测试，并同步更新
README、architecture、roadmap 和 test-assets 文档。

refactor(test): 按需初始化 runtime 并均衡 nextest 测试分组 (#22)

## Why
- 现有测试运行时间主要被语言 runtime 的首次全量初始化放大，单个轻量测试也会为 80+ runtime 付出冷启动成本。
- `nextest` 下的 fixture / showcase 聚合测试分组不均衡，关键路径被少数大测试拖长，影响日常开发反馈速度。

## What
- 将语言 runtime 从首次全量初始化改为按需初始化，并把仅用于判断可用性的路径改成只查静态 descriptor，避免无谓编译。
- 将 `fixture` 与 `showcase` 聚合测试按家族拆分，并补充覆盖校验，确保新增样例不会漏进任何测试组。
- 保持 `CI`、`prek` 和 `just test` 继续统一使用 `nextest`，不改变自动化入口语义。

## Testing
- `just test`
- `cargo nextest run --config-file .config/nextest.toml --cargo-quiet
--failure-output final --no-tests pass -j 8`
- `cargo test --lib
tests::bash_highlights_shebang_special_variables_regex_and_parameters --
--exact`
- `cargo test --lib
tests::github_actions_advanced_workflow_inherits_default_shell_and_highlights_nested_expressions
-- --exact`
- `cargo test --lib language_runtime::tests`

## Notes
- 本地当前分支下 `just test` 的 `nextest` summary 约为 `5.6s`，相比最初那次约 `117s`
的样本已经显著下降。

commit.message:
fix(desktop): bypass local browser auth for desktop runtime

Summary:
- set a desktop-only environment flag on the Electron-launched
  `openase all-in-one` child process
- skip wiring the human browser auth service when that desktop flag is
  present so the local loopback runtime opens straight into the
  workspace
- add focused desktop unit tests and app-level flag parsing tests for
  the new auth bypass path

Rationale:
- desktop users were landing on the local bootstrap/login gate even for
  a packaged app that owns the loopback runtime on the same machine
- limiting the bypass to the desktop-spawned child process avoids
  changing the normal CLI and server authentication behavior

Tests:
- `go test ./internal/app`
- `corepack pnpm --dir desktop exec vitest run tests/unit/service-process.test.js tests/integration/service-lifecycle.test.js`
- packaged desktop smoke with fresh PostgreSQL config and verified
  `/api/v1/auth/session` returned `login_required=false`

commit.message:
fix(render): 修复嵌套 overlay 退化并统一 block 几何 (#18)

## Why
- `worker-configuration.d.ts` 这类大型 TypeScript 声明文件会在嵌套 overlay
合并阶段出现严重性能退化，看起来像卡死。
- 前一轮 render 优化暴露了两个视觉问题：多行注释跨行样式会丢失，嵌套 block 的几何又会被宿主包装行放大，导致 raw
string / JSDoc 这类区域的 block tint 范围不稳定。
- 日常调试时还需要显式写 `cargo run --bin kat`，命令有些啰嗦。

## What
- 把 `overlay_nested_region` 和 `overlay_style_spans` 改成基于已排序 spans 的线性
merge，避免对每个 injected region 全量重建整份 spans。
- 修正 `render_styled_spans` 的按行 span 切片逻辑，保留跨多行 span 在后续行上的活跃状态。
- 把嵌套注入区域的视觉策略收口到 runtime 统一推导：独立的多行 block 会自动拿到 block tint，显式 inline
注入继续保持透明叠加。
- 在 block 几何推导里区分 `content` / `blank` / `wrapper` 行；wrapper 行不再参与 block
的纵向或横向范围，宿主定界符也不会再被 block tint 包进去。
- 保留轻量版 `KAT_DEBUG_PROGRESS` 阶段日志，并把 `Cargo.toml` 的默认运行目标设成 `kat`。
- 补充回归测试，覆盖 overlay 合并语义、多行注释跨行样式、JSDoc block tint、inline template 注入保持
inline，以及 Rust raw-string SQL 的 block 几何。

## Testing
- `KAT_AGENT_TEST_LOG_MODE=quiet just test`
- `cargo run --release --
/Users/dcjanus/Code/hacknews-feeds/worker-configuration.d.ts --paging
never --debug-timing`
- `cargo run --release -- testdata/showcase/rust/injections.rs --paging
never --debug-timing`
